reviews.review_description:
I have Stalingrad and it’s awesome. But being the Americans in d-day would be a whole lot funner. 

Once the major upd comes out I will definitely buy this one

Great game I’ve played the demo but once it upd that’s gonna change :)

developer_response.body:
Thank you! 

Good news — the major update is now live and ready to play !

If you enjoyed the demo, we'd love to hear what you think of the full version. See you on the beaches of Normandy, soldier! 🫡

reviews.review_description:
This is nothing that really happen I don't think but maybe make a under water or a under ground fight please i think it would be fun and thank you if you do make it or someone else 
and tell me if you do pls

developer_response.body:
That's a very unique suggestion! 😄🫡

An underwater mission with combat divers could actually be really interesting. There were special operations involving combat swimmers and underwater sabotage during World War II, so it's not as far-fetched as it sounds. 😄

Thanks for the unusual idea — we always enjoy hearing suggestions like this! 👍

reviews.review_title: BEST D DAY GAME ON VR
reviews.review_description: This is my favorite ww2 game ever. And the new update looks so great, and the gameplay is great. They added a sten gun,  MP-28, 1918, and many more new guns I love. I’ve only played the demo version, and in the future I might buy the full version.   if you could make a ww1 game too, I’d play it in a heartbeat. Totally recommend playing, best. WW2 game. ever

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much! 😄

We're really happy to hear that you enjoyed the Full Release update !

Thanks for the amazing review, and we're glad the game left such a good impression on you 😉

reviews.review_description: Beautifully created very worth it even with the new update i believe something like japan would be amazing next it would be very neat to see how it would come out

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much! 😄🙌

And as for what's next... we'll be announcing our future plans very soon

reviews.review_description: The gameplay and story are excellent but I would like if you guys made the game more realistic instead of the cartoonish or blocky characters you guys have. Try to make it so the game looks closer to real life. Thank you.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for giving the game a chance! 😄

We're glad to hear you enjoyed the gameplay and story.

As for the visuals, the art style is a deliberate creative choice and an important part of the game's identity. It's not necessarily better or worse than a realistic style — it's simply the style we chose for this project.

That said, who knows? Maybe one day we'll create a game with a more realistic visual style  😄


Thanks again for playing and for sharing your feedback!

reviews.review_description: The update was great but I do have 1 complaint when the shotgun is held with two hands it is sideways and I have to twist my controllers to make it right side up, but the shotgun should not hold ￼this game back from five stars

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the positive feedback! 

And thanks for pointing that out about the shotgun. We'll take a closer look at it and see what's going on there 

We're glad you enjoyed the update!

reviews.review_description: Just perfect

developer_response.body:
hank you so much for the 5/5 review! 😄🫡

We're really glad you're enjoying the game.

reviews.review_title: Great but needs a few touches.
reviews.review_description: Love it, but let us finish D day.

developer_response.body:
Thank you! 😊

The demo is meant to give players a taste of the atmosphere and gameplay from the full version. If you'd like to continue the story and finish Omaha Beach, we invite you to check out the full game 😉

reviews.review_description:
the game is great and theres always fun to play but still pls add more levels than 2 and there neads to be where if you kill them you can get tnere gun . Pls add limited ammo and you can get more from kills and finding it all over the map. Pls add a level where you go on the boats then arive at the beach then shoot all the bunkers down so basicly the proper dday level. Add multiplayer and add cars planes and controleble tanks add more bolt action rifles last but not least add new tools like new grenads new healing tools and stuff like binoclulers and teliscopes to see far away
but still a great big thank yo to making this gam😃😃👍👍👍

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for all the awesome feedback! 😄
The full version of the game is available on the store, and we're currently working on a huge overhaul of D-Day with lots of improvements and new content.

And if you enjoyed D-Day, feel free to check out Stalingrad as well — it's our newer game

reviews.review_description: Intense but really fun and for some reason I can’t get past “the road” level ( its not a skill issue it’s just I think they are still testing it)

developer_response.body:
Thanks! Glad you’re enjoying it!
About “The Road” — that’s just a demo, not the full game. We do have a full version (and we’re currently working on a huge update for it).

If you don’t want to buy anything right now, feel free to try Days of Heroes: Stalingrad DEMO — just type it in the Meta Store search. It’s our newer project and gives you a lot of fun completely for free! 🚀

reviews.review_description: Your game is good but the one thing that’s about about it is the weapon variety you need to add like more weapons that you can unlock such as the more of the more enemies you kill the more guns you would unlock maybe add MP40 but besides that it’s a pretty good game

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the comment! 🙌
Just to clarify — this is only the demo version 😉
The full game includes a lot more weapons, missions, and action to enjoy!

reviews.review_description: i love this but i want to have more levels

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much — we’re really happy to hear that you love the game! ❤️

If you’re looking for more content, we’ve got good news:

There is a full version of Days of Heroes: D-Day with more levels.

We’ve also released a newer game, Days of Heroes: Stalingrad.

We hope you’ll enjoy diving deeper into the Days of Heroes universe.

Have a great time playing!

reviews.review_description: It’s a good game in the first level do not disturb the peace as soon as I heard boss music I was LIkE HECK NAh and the enemy brought out there big guns lol. Good music on the bossfight. Also I have a suggestion can you add war music to the game on do not disturb  the peace level  there’s only music when tank arrives then it’s over there is also no music in the 2nd level completely

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the feedback!
We just released the second part of the game — Days of Heroes: Stalingrad (there’s also a free demo if you want to try it out). We’re also preparing updates for D-Day, so all suggestions are welcome. Thank you!

reviews.review_description: I love it I wish more people knew about this game kind of reminds me of call of duty I wish there was more levels and maybe multiplayer

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your positive comment! 
We’ve got some good news for you: the full versions of Days of Heroes: D-Day and our brand-new Days of Heroes: Stalingrad are now available in the store! 🚀

reviews.review_description: You never stop moving that’s what I love about it and reloading

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot!  Glad you’re enjoying it. If you’re interested, make sure to check out the free demo of Days of Heroes: Stalingrad too — it’s also free! 🎮

reviews.review_description: So far, this game is awesome, but what we need so very badly is co-op. Not just multiplayer but enemies that vary on players, a scoreboard, proximity chat, and everything alike. This game is so fun, and multiplayer would just quadruple to replayability. Go dev!

developer_response.body:
Hey! Really appreciate the kind words and your feedback 🙌
Right now, we’re fully focused on developing the next game in the series — Stalingrad — and bringing in brand new mechanics and experiences.

But co-op and features like proximity chat and scaling enemies are definitely ideas we’re keeping in mind for the future.
Thanks for being part of the journey — it means a lot! 💪🔥

reviews.review_description: Loved it would love more missions I only had 2 available and the second didn’t have an ending for some reason unless I did it wrong would also love better character texture and weapon reload realistic but overall good game keep up the hard work would also love a German perspective.

developer_response.body:
Hey! Thanks so much for the positive feedback — we really appreciate it! 🙏

What you played was just the demo version of Days of Heroes: D-Day — the full game is available on the Meta Store with many more missions.

Also, we’re excited to share that next month we’re launching Days of Heroes: Stalingrad — a full new chapter where you’ll be able to fight on both sides, including from the German perspective.

Thanks again for the kind words and your support — it keeps us going! 💪
🛒 Full game: https://www.meta.com/experiences/days-of-heroes-d-day/5680842148663882

reviews.review_description: All though the game is great and I enjoy the gameplay but it is lacking in other aspects of the game like multiplayer and more maps and I have also experienced a glitch where I have finished a level but the game doesn’t end and some of the maps are missing the floor in areas

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your feedback — we really appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts! 🙏
We’re currently working on a brand-new game in the series: Days of Heroes: Stalingrad. Once it’s released, we’ll be coming back to D-Day to polish, improve, and expand it based on community input — including issues like the ones you mentioned.

We’d love to have you on our Discord, where players share ideas and help us shape the future of the series:
👉 www.dohvr.com/discord/

Thanks again for playing and for supporting Days of Heroes!

reviews.review_description: After i was done with the road when i finish that level i didn't see the next mission please help me!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review!
This is a demo version. You will find more missions in the full version
If you still need help, please visit our Discord server: www.dohvr.com/discord/

reviews.review_title: One of the best WW2 games ever
reviews.review_description: Hey, I absolutely love your game 10 out of 10 all around. I will say, however, I think it would be more interesting. If you were able to perhaps grab your fellow allies. It dragged them to safety if they are injured it could make a better gaming experience like how they use to in real World War II. Not only would this make it even cooler it could perhaps have the mechanics even better but overall one of the best games I’ve ever played in VR and cannot wait until the full thing is released keep staying awesome👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the kind words and thoughtful suggestion — we love hearing ideas like this! 🙌
Good news: the full version of Days of Heroes: D-Day is already available on the Meta Store!
Even better news: we're getting close to launching the next entry in the series — Days of Heroes: Stalingrad — with much more advanced systems and modernized mechanics.

We’d love to hear more from you and other fans over on our Discord:
👉 www.dohvr.com/discord/

Thanks again for your support

reviews.review_description:
I played this game for like 7 minutes (because it was near my bedtime) and it felt like an hour with how much fun I was having! I agree with having proximity chat, and co-op, and I also agree with having 2 teams duke it out, with each team getting points, that they can spend as a team on different buffs. The buffs could include, tanks, transport vehicles, air strikes, artillery, defenses, and more! I recommend the price at around $10 
 (so I can afford it🤣🤣🤣).

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the awesome feedback! We’re really glad you enjoyed Days of Heroes: D-Day.

Days of Heroes: D-Day is available now on the Meta Store for $15, but keep an eye out—we regularly run promotions where you can snag it for around $10.

Plus, Days of Heroes: Stalingrad is coming soon—and just like D‑Day, it will launch with a free demo alongside the full paid version. Even if you’re not ready to buy, you’ll still be able to jump in and play the demo for free.

Join our community and stay up to date on all the latest news and giveaways on Discord: 👉 www.dohvr.com/discord/

Thanks again for your support—we can’t wait to share what’s next!

reviews.review_description: very good game you should try it out and it's the best they need a make the full game because it's so fun.

developer_response.body:
Great news! 🎉
The full version of D-Day is already available on the Meta Store!
Even better — we’ve also recently released Days of Heroes: Stalingrad, and right now we’re working on a huge update for D-Day.

Thank you so much for your comment and support — it really means a lot! 🙏
You can find all the store links (and even a free demo for Stalingrad!) here: 👉 www.dohvr.com

reviews.review_description: Honestly disappointed, the game is just bad

developer_response.body: Since your review was posted, we have released a major update that includes a large amount of new content, improvements, and fixes. We’d love to invite you to give the game another try and see how it feels now.

reviews.review_description: For the low low cost of free I had a lot of fun with this. It’s only one mission but that makes sense since it’s a demo. If you are bored one day and want something to have fun with then this is definitely a great option.

played the mobile version and this feels very similar but there are a few things that need to be improved. the main thing I've noticed is reloading is very clunky on the quest 2, your constantly hitting the controllers together to try reload. Its could be great but there's room for a lot of improvement!

Also these guys made a mobile game and thats where the trailer footage comes from so if anyone is looking for what to expect, I would recommend it.

This game is fun. ive played the demo level alone like 10 times. though there are a few issues. first off is the reloading. the magazine isn't automatically inserted into the gun when its close. you have to let go of the magazine for it to insert. Second is the guns feel un natural to hold. your second hand has to grab the gun way too far up. the mp40 and 1911 are the only exeptions (and the slides sometimes dont work). And my third and final issue is some times you get killed for what feels like no reason. just barely peeking you head out of cover can be a death sentence. 
Since this is a demo im sure these issues will be fixed. like i said, the game has a lot of potential and i hope the dev continues working on the game.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your comment!
You can play the full version here: 
https://www.oculus.com/experiences/quest/5680842148663882/

reviews.review_title: pass play maybe once its done

reviews.review_title: Medal of honor for the quest. I guess.
reviews.review_description: This game is a lot of fun even though the graphics aren't realistic. There is currently only a tutorial and one short level available but this is a demo so its not all that surprising. I think that this game has a lot of potential and I will keep coming back to it as long as we actually get a campaign of some sort. Or just more missions. Also I noticed that others have talked about the Omaha beach landing mission that needs to be in this since you know it is called D-DAY. Any way, the full game is available on the rift platform so basically right now you need a rift or VR desktop to play full version of this. Because of this I'm downgrading my review to 4 stars but again this is a demo so I don't know what the hell i expected.

reviews.review_title: Fun  for a minute, but one huge problem
reviews.review_description: I’m actually angry only the demo on App Lab, and then the full game on Rift. So how do I get the full game on Quest? Game is fun seriously needs a lot of work though. Absolutely no way to get the full game on quest unless you have a computer. So kinda defeats the purpose. The whole point of App Lab is to be able to do more without a computer. At this point this game doesn’t deserve to even be on here. Can’t stand when companies pull this. It’s been on App Lab for a long time now, and not one thing done, and no full version available. 1 star is actually pushing it. My true rating would be a -5 stars.

developer_response.body: Since your review was posted, we have released a major update that includes a large amount of new content, improvements, and fixes. We’d love to invite you to give the game another try and see how it feels now.

reviews.review_description:
I couldn't find smooth turning anywhere, and I look everywhere. This ruined the experience and gave me a headache. I cannot deal with non-smooth turning, it always gives me a headache and I always immediately look for a way to turn it on everytime I boot up a new game.
If there is smooth turning and I just didn't find it I will be a very happy boy but at the moment I am a very unhappy boy.

I think the idea of this game is quite nice, not many VR shooter games focus on WW2. I have played the demo atleast 5 times now, and really enjowed it! But it has many draw backs that could be easliy fixed. For starters, picking up stuff, like from your inventaory is really clunky, and hard to pick stuff up. Reloading is also an issue too, which the mag doesnt really fit inside the gun, unless your perfectly line it up. I know this is a demo, but with a little bit of fixing some things, I think this would be a great game.
Ok it’s a good game but it said full version and not demo so I will say ok but it was abandoned
This game is fun but it needs improving with the weapons for example it’s hard for me to hold on to a weapon and it’s annoying to see only one map 🤒
Cant wait for more content to come out
its hard to explain how bad this game is

The game is incredibly fun and the environment is very well done for the art style. Hand tracking needs some improvement. Guns are
A bit clunky to reload and the rifles (2 handed guns) are very particular about where you have to place your hands which caused some issues with gameplay as you have to adjust your hand position to grip the weapon. Otherwise I think this will be a great game.

Thanks so much for the kind words and support! 🙏
We’re really glad you enjoyed the demo — and if you get the chance, the full Days of Heroes: D-Day offers a lot more action! 
You can also check out Days of Heroes: Stalingrad, our newer and even more advanced release. 🎯

reviews.review_description: Cant wait for more. Sad when it ended

reviews.review_title: I thought it was fun but...
reviews.review_description: I loved the idea, and WW2 weapons have a special place in my heart, but I had a few issues. I felt like it was too hard to grab things on your chest. I would try to grab a new mag/clip and it would grab my rifle instead, or I would try to grab the bandage and it would grab my pistol instead. I would either spread everything out more on your chest, or get rid of the air grab thing and make it so you have to actually grab everything with out the star wars force grab thing. Super fun, and looking forward to the full game. Cant wait for a VR D-day.

developer_response.body: Since your review was posted, we have released a major update that includes a large amount of new content, improvements, and fixes. We’d love to invite you to give the game another try and see how it feels now.

it rely god my daddy let me play and i lovd kiling the natze scum

reviews.review_description:
So I recently beat don’t rest in peace in hardcore mode and how many times I died and lost thanks to my guns dropping when I paused to talk to family then me not being able to pick them back up thanks to my character picking literally everything else up. But apart from that. I LOVE this game. I love how the shooting work. I love how the sniper works. I love how you can try and kill every with the axe in the log.  I love the push that you do to get to the checkpoints. I love the map. I love the fact there’s supplies and guns put around adding to reply value. I love the atmosphere but the fact grabbing stuff doesn’t work half the time makes this game really annoying to play(it doesn’t help that it’s quite hard in general with you dying in one or two hits) 

If you’re looking at getting the game while it’s free. Get it. I recommend it if you don’t mind dying a few times thanks to glitchy motion controls. 

I hope you improve the grabbing and making it more easily able to grab objects on the ground even if it is funny for other people watching you try to pick something up from the ground in the game

reviews.review_title: Needs a significant amount of work
reviews.review_description:
There are a lot of things that need reworked for this game to be any good: I wonder how this got approved for App Lab at all. 

#1: Controls

Oh God, where do I start? The use of stick movement doesn’t synergize with the combat system very well: it lets you move too quickly for it to emphasize using cover. Grabbing weapons and using your inventory is extremely unreliable, and two-handing rifles doesn’t work very well. Hand interaction also needs major work due to the grabbing pose looking like a thumbs up, and they look like paddles, not hands. Also, there’s no penalty for one-handing weapons outside of the sniper rifle, so there’s no incentive to actually try aiming. 

#2: Level and Enemy Design

There’s only three enemy types in the first mission: problem is, there’s effectively no difference in how you respond between Officers and Privates: they’re basically the same enemy type. The first tank placement in Mission 1 is also poorly placed and colored, as I had no idea where it was until I saw the yellow bullets flying from it, and having died several times from objects I had no idea where they were coming from. Also, the old “run past everything” trick is too effective in many situations. 

#3: Presentation

I won’t criticize the use of a low-poly art style: the problem is, here it’s used in a way that doesn’t work to the game’s benefit, as it makes the action less clear for a shooter of this type. It also isn’t well-optimized: the tutorial has major performance issues. 


This game clearly feels like it was rushed out the door and too much time was spent on fixing bugs instead of making a good shooter: D E L A Y THE FULL GAME. By at least a year. Please.

reviews.review_title: Fun but some things to work on
reviews.review_description: First of all I hope the devs are working hard on this cause it is a cool concept needs work with gun mechanics and stuff and I hope when there is a full game you have voice actors and a story based events in different battles I think with a devoted dev this could be the game everyone wants to try
I have replayed and replayed the demo so many times and cant wait for the full release

The first thing that is very minor is there are quite a few grammar and spelling errors in the text. Second thing is that when you die it just splashes a bland "You Died" screen and you respawn. Another thing, probably the biggest problem, is that grabbing weapons is awkward and reloading pistols is very hard as your controller gets in the way of the slide on the pistol. Also when reloading you drop the magazine/clip into the gun whereas it should just go in or do a guided slightly animated reload. I love WW2 so I hope this game gets polished up. The environments while simple are surprisingly interesting, I love seeing all the historical guns in 3D including the tanks and finally, apart from the reloads, shooting feels responsive and powerful (though sometimes when I shoot an enemies head it just staggers them, that should be a one shot kill) I never wrote a review before but this game has so much potential and will buy the finished game if the listed problems are adressed.
